authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

reaches.name: BLM Springs portion
reaches.fromDesc: The portions of Fish Slough which lie within the BLM Springs (BLM-managed spring pools within the Fish Slough Area of Critical Environmental Concern / Ecological Reserve)

reaches.name: All other portions of Fish Slough
reaches.fromDesc: All portions of Fish Slough outside the BLM Springs closure area

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(49) — Fish Slough (Mono Co.)
sources.quotedText: (49) Fish Slough (Mono Co.). (A) The portions of Fish Slough which lie within the BLM Springs. Closed to all fishing all year. (B) All other portions of Fish Slough. Also, see Section 5.00(b)(10) for black bass regulations. All year. 5 trout

sources.title: BLM — Fish Slough Area of Critical Environmental Concern
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 260158, "Fish Slough", Stream, Mono/Inyo Co., CA
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":260158,"gaz_name":"Fish Slough","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","fcode":46000,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"Mono"/"Inyo"} — geometry multipoint; mouth point (Web Mercator -13180643.559, 4498581.7633) converts to lon -118.403723, lat 37.425485 (Inyo Co. end, tributary of Owens River), matching the HomeTownLocator GNIS mirror for this feature id and replacing the file's previous coordinate (-118.4009, 37.4691), which was off by ~4.86 km.

parameters.note: Portions of Fish Slough within the BLM Springs (protected spring pools managed by BLM/CDFW for the endangered Owens pupfish and other native fishes) are closed to all fishing all year.

regulations.humanSummary: The portions of Fish Slough within the BLM Springs are closed to all fishing year-round.
regulations.verbatimText: The portions of Fish Slough which lie within the BLM Springs. Closed to all fishing all year.

parameters.note: Applies to all portions of Fish Slough outside the closed BLM Springs area.

regulations.humanSummary: All other portions of Fish Slough are open to fishing year-round.
regulations.verbatimText: All other portions of Fish Slough. Also, see Section 5.00(b)(10) for black bass regulations. All year. 5 trout

parameters.counting_rule: The table entry sets a 5-trout daily bag limit for the open (non-BLM-Springs) portions of Fish Slough; per 14 CCR 7.50(a)(4), unless otherwise provided it is unlawful to possess more than one daily bag limit, so the possession limit also equals 5.

regulations.humanSummary: 5-trout daily bag and possession limit on the open portions of Fish Slough.
regulations.verbatimText: All other portions of Fish Slough. Also, see Section 5.00(b)(10) for black bass regulations. All year. 5 trout

parameters.description: Black bass on Fish Slough are governed separately by the statewide black bass regulations table at 14 CCR §5.00(b)(10), not by this trout-specific entry.

regulations.humanSummary: Black bass fishing on Fish Slough is governed by the separate statewide black bass table (§5.00(b)(10)), cross-referenced from this entry rather than restated here.
regulations.verbatimText: Also, see Section 5.00(b)(10) for black bass regulations.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
